message:
fix(runtime_math): move DecisionCardRingBuffer + EvidenceRingBuffer slots to Vec

Both ring buffers stored their per-slot AtomicU64/seqno cells in
[DecisionCardSlot; CAP] / [EvidenceSlot; CAP] inline arrays —
materialized at construction via core::array::from_fn().

That works for the typical CAP=64 case but blows the stack at the
larger CAP values used by the membrane's evidence/decision tracker
in some test harness configurations: each slot carries a sizeable
metadata payload + AtomicU64s, and an N×slot inline array bottoms
out the test thread's stack on construction (manifesting as a
spurious "stack overflow" from inside Default::default(), not from
any test logic).

Switched to Vec<DecisionCardSlot> / Vec<EvidenceSlot>:

- new() pre-sizes with Vec::with_capacity(CAP) and pushes CAP
  default slots in a tight loop. CAP is still the const-generic
  type parameter, so the public API is unchanged — all callers
  that wrote DecisionCardRingBuffer::<64>::new() keep working.
- The slots are heap-allocated once at construction; subsequent
  index-into-slots traffic in publish/load paths is identical to
  the old &slots[i] form.
- Default::default() routes through new() so a:
    let buf = DecisionCardRingBuffer::<1024>::default();
  no longer materializes a 1024-slot inline array on the stack.

Test-artifact refresh that goes with the code change:

- tests/runtime_math/admission_gate_report.v1.json
- tests/runtime_math/controller_ablation_report.v1.json
- tests/runtime_math/controller_manifest.v1.json
  Each carried a generated_at timestamp and a couple of derived
  fields (per-slot fingerprints) that move when the buffers are
  constructed via the new path. Re-running the runtime_math
  harness emits the refreshed reports; behavior contracts (gate
  decisions, controller weights, manifest checksums of the
  underlying decision data) are unchanged — only the metadata
  fields tied to slot construction move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
